# Implement Predictive Scheduling with Machine Learning

## Description
Add machine learning capabilities to predict user scheduling preferences and suggest optimal times for events based on historical patterns.

## Type
- **Type:** New Feature
- **Priority:** High
- **Component:** AI Agent, Machine Learning
- **Milestone:** AI Agent v2.0

## Use Case
The AI learns that a user typically schedules team meetings on Tuesday mornings and suggests "Tuesday at 9am" when the user says "Schedule a team meeting this week".

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Analyze historical scheduling patterns
- [ ] Predict optimal times for different event types
- [ ] Learn user preferences for specific contacts/locations
- [ ] Suggest recurring patterns based on usage
- [ ] Adapt to changing preferences over time
- [ ] Provide confidence scores for predictions
- [ ] Handle multiple family members' patterns

## Technical Requirements
- [ ] Implement ML model for pattern recognition
- [ ] Create user preference learning system
- [ ] Add prediction API endpoints
- [ ] Store and analyze scheduling history
- [ ] Implement preference feedback system
- [ ] Add privacy controls for data collection
- [ ] Create prediction accuracy metrics

## Implementation Notes
- Use TensorFlow.js or similar for client-side ML
- Consider privacy-first approach with local processing
- Implement gradual learning to avoid overfitting
- Add user controls for data collection preferences
- Consider federated learning for privacy
